name: Defense & Aerospace Sector
summary: Counterintuitively, defense stocks are FALLING during the war. ITA (Defense ETF) -11% monthly, -1.9% today. Lockheed Martin -3.5% monthly. The initial war spike has reversed as markets price in: (1) Pentagon $200B supplemental facing GOP resistance, (2) broader equity risk-off dragging all sectors when VIX >30, (3) market distinguishing between commodity beneficiaries (energy) and government-contract beneficiaries (defense). Indian defense (HAL, BEL) showing similar pattern.

signals.content: ITA -1.9% today, -11% monthly — defense is selling off DURING a war. Lockheed -1.8%. This divergence from energy (XLE +1.7%) shows the market distinguishing between commodity beneficiaries and government-contract beneficiaries. Pentagon's $200B supplemental request facing GOP resistance. When VIX >30, correlation goes to 1 and even 'war winners' sell in the liquidity grab. (Sources: yfinance, Washington Post, CNBC)

edges.context: Defense stocks depend on Pentagon budget. $200B supplemental facing political resistance. Budget uncertainty > war premium.
Defense is an S&P sector. When VIX >30, all sectors correlate. Defense premium from war is overwhelmed by broad risk-off.

trigger_points.mechanism: Approval would remove the budget uncertainty weighing on defense stocks. ITA would likely spike 5-10% on approval. Rejection would confirm the underperformance.
